description:
## Problem
`FaviconActor.valid_image_data?` (line 152) only rejects HTML/XML content strings. It does not validate actual image format magic bytes:
- PNG: `\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n`
- JPEG: `\xFF\xD8\xFF`
- ICO: `\x00\x00\x01\x00` (or `\x00\x00\x02\x00`)
- SVG: already handled by the HTML/XML check
- WebP: `RIFF....WEBP`

A corrupt or malicious response with random binary data would pass validation and be saved as a favicon file.

## Fix
Add magic byte checks for PNG, JPEG, ICO, and WebP formats. Keep the existing HTML/XML rejection. Return true only if the data matches a known image format header.

## File
`src/favicon_storage.cr` — `valid_image_data?` class method
title: Improve valid_image_data? with magic byte validation
